text:
In software development, Git branching helps teams work efficiently on the same project. Instead of editing the main code directly, developers create separate feature branches. Each branch is like a private workspace for a new feature or fix.

Once a feature is complete, the developer submits a pull request. This asks teammates to review the changes before adding them to the main project. After approval, the changes are merged into the main branch. Merging combines the separate work without losing any updates.

This version control workflow keeps the main code stable. Multiple people can try new ideas on branches without causing conflicts. When everyone follows these steps, collaboration becomes smooth and organised.
